name: SV40 Polio Vaccines
description: The SV40 Polio Vaccines conspiracy theory centers on the claim that certain batches of polio vaccines administered in the United States between 1955 and 1963 (and in some other countries) were contaminated with Simian Virus 40 (SV40), a virus found in rhesus macaque monkeys, whose kidneys were used to produce the vaccines. Proponents argue that this contamination has led to increased rates of certain cancers, particularly mesothelioma, brain tumors, and bone cancers. The theory originated in the late 1990s and early 2000s, fueled by research suggesting the presence of SV40 DNA in some human tumors. Key evidence cited includes studies finding SV40 DNA sequences in tumor samples and epidemiological data allegedly showing a correlation between SV40 exposure and cancer incidence. Mainstream science acknowledges that some polio vaccines were indeed contaminated with SV40. However, the prevailing scientific consensus, based on extensive research, is that there is no conclusive evidence of a causal link between SV40 exposure from the vaccines and increased cancer rates. Many studies have failed to replicate the initial findings, and methodological limitations in early research have been identified. Furthermore, the ongoing presence of SV40 DNA in some tumors is attributed to other potential sources of exposure, such as undetected natural transmission from monkeys or laboratory contamination. The controversy persists, fueled by public distrust in pharmaceutical companies and government agencies, and concerns about vaccine safety.
subtitle: Polio vaccine contamination and cancer claims

claims.claim: Polio vaccines administered between 1955 and 1963 were contaminated with SV40.
claims.evidence: Documented evidence of SV40 presence in early polio vaccine batches.
claims.criticism: Acknowledged as factual by mainstream science; affected batches were withdrawn.

claims.claim: SV40 contamination from the vaccines has caused increased rates of certain cancers in humans.
claims.evidence: Studies finding SV40 DNA in human tumor samples and epidemiological correlations.
claims.criticism: Mainstream science finds no conclusive causal link; correlation does not equal causation; methodological flaws in early studies; alternative SV40 exposure routes possible.

claims.claim: Government and pharmaceutical companies have covered up the link between SV40 and cancer.
claims.evidence: Alleged suppression of research findings and downplaying of risks.
claims.criticism: Lack of concrete evidence; transparency in scientific literature; independent research efforts contradicting the claim of a causal link.

keyFigures.name: Michele Carbone
keyFigures.role: Researcher who published studies linking SV40 to mesothelioma.

keyFigures.name: Howard Strickler
keyFigures.role: Epidemiologist who conducted studies finding no increased cancer risk from SV40-contaminated vaccines.

mythologyConnections: The Fall (loss of innocence through contamination)

timeline.event: Development and mass administration of polio vaccines.
Discovery of SV40 in monkey kidney cells.
Publication of studies linking SV40 to human cancers, fueling the conspiracy theory.
